rawText:
Artificial intelligence has transformed industries from finance to health care, but several stubborn gaps are proving difficult to close. One of the most consequential affects the hundreds of millions of people who do not speak English. Research published in late 2025 found that the accuracy of leading language models in non-English languages was between twelve and twenty-nine percentage points lower than in English, with some models scoring as low as twenty-three percent on queries posed in African languages. Because models break text into small units called tokens, and that process is optimized for English, the same question can cost up to five times more to process in a language like Yoruba.

The language gap carries real-world stakes. A fifty-million-dollar initiative to deploy AI tools in a thousand primary health clinics across Africa risks offering inferior medical advice simply because patients speak local languages. Even explicitly multilingual models often retrieve facts internally in English and translate only at the final step, introducing additional errors at every stage. Fine-tuning with small amounts of high-quality non-English data can improve accuracy by more than five percentage points, but the dominant economic incentives still favor English-first development.

A parallel limitation is emerging in hardware. The shift from training AI models to running them in real time, a process known as inference, is exposing the weaknesses of current processors. Computing performance has roughly tripled every few years, but off-chip memory bandwidth has improved by only a factor of about 1.6, creating what engineers call a memory wall. Several startups are now designing chips that tackle this bottleneck through larger on-chip memory, in-memory computing, or architectures tailored specifically to transformer models, though the pace of algorithmic change means today's specialized hardware could quickly become obsolete.

Meanwhile, in India's outsourcing sector, which generated more than three hundred and fifteen billion dollars in combined revenue this year, the feared mass automation of coding has not materialized. Deploying AI tools in legacy software environments with poor documentation and intertwined systems remains far harder than building new applications from scratch. Outsourcing firms are repositioning as strategic AI consultants rather than code factories, and their hiring has continued despite widespread predictions of displacement.

The common thread across these domains is that artificial intelligence excels in controlled, English-language, greenfield environments but struggles with the messy complexity of the real world. Closing these gaps will require not just better models but more diverse training data, more efficient hardware, and sustained investment in the populations and systems that current technology serves least well.

overview.title: The Gaps AI Still Cannot Close

sentences.original: Artificial intelligence has transformed industries from finance to health care, but several stubborn gaps are proving difficult to close.
sentences.translation: 인공지능은 금융에서 의료까지 산업을 변혁시켰지만, 몇 가지 완고한 격차는 좁히기 어려운 것으로 드러나고 있다.

grammarElements.text: industries from finance to health care
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + from A to B 범위 표현

grammarElements.text: but several stubborn gaps are proving difficult to close
grammarElements.label: but 대조절 (prove + 형용사)

vocabulary.example: The stubborn inflation rate refused to respond to rate hikes.
The merger is proving more complicated than expected.

expressions.usage: 예상보다 해결이 어려운 문제를 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Technology] has transformed [broad range], but [specific problems] are proving difficult to [solve]
patterns.explanation: 기술의 성과를 인정한 뒤 but으로 남은 과제를 제시하는 도입 패턴
examples: Automation has transformed manufacturing from assembly to packaging, but quality control is proving difficult to automate.
Digital banking has transformed payments from retail to wholesale, but fraud detection is proving difficult to perfect.

drillQuestions.question: Several stubborn gaps are ______ difficult to close.

sentences.notes: 도입부에서 AI의 성과를 인정하면서도 but으로 글의 핵심 주제인 '한계'를 제시하는 균형 잡힌 서두이다.

koreanAlignment.en: has transformed industries from finance to health care
several stubborn gaps
are proving difficult to close

sentences.original: One of the most consequential affects the hundreds of millions of people who do not speak English.
sentences.translation: 가장 중대한 격차 중 하나는 영어를 사용하지 않는 수억 명의 사람들에게 영향을 미친다.

grammarElements.text: the hundreds of millions of people who do not speak English
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: This may be the most consequential policy decision of the decade.

expressions.usage: 여러 문제 중 특히 영향력이 큰 것을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: One of the most [superlative adjective] [verbs] [affected group]
patterns.explanation: 여러 사례 중 가장 심각한 것을 선별하여 부각시키는 패턴
examples: One of the most overlooked affects rural communities with no broadband access.
One of the most damaging affects small businesses that lack cybersecurity resources.

drillQuestions.question: One of the most ______ gaps affects people who do not speak English.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 'gaps'를 받아 가장 중대한 하나를 선택하여 논점을 좁히는 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: One of the most consequential
affects the hundreds of millions of people
who do not speak English

sentences.original: Research published in late 2025 found that the accuracy of leading language models in non-English languages was between twelve and twenty-nine percentage points lower than in English, with some models scoring as low as twenty-three percent on queries posed in African languages.
sentences.translation: 2025년 말에 발표된 연구에 따르면, 주요 언어 모델의 비영어권 언어 정확도는 영어보다 12~29퍼센트포인트 낮았으며, 일부 모델은 아프리카 언어로 된 질의에서 23퍼센트라는 낮은 점수를 기록했다.

grammarElements.text: the accuracy of leading language models in non-English languages
grammarElements.label: that절 내 주어

grammarElements.text: was between twelve and twenty-nine percentage points lower than in English
grammarElements.label: 비교급 보어

grammarElements.text: with some models scoring as low as twenty-three percent
grammarElements.label: with + 현재분사 부대상황

vocabulary.example: Unemployment fell by two percentage points last quarter.
The system processes thousands of queries per second.

expressions.usage: 수치가 예상보다 훨씬 낮음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Research published in [date] found that [metric] was [number] lower than [benchmark], with some [subset] scoring as low as [floor]
patterns.explanation: 연구 결과를 인용하며 평균과 최저치를 함께 제시하는 데이터 보고 패턴
examples: Research published in 2024 found that rural broadband speeds were 40% lower than urban averages, with some regions scoring as low as 5 Mbps.
Research published last year found that student performance was 15 points lower than the national benchmark, with some schools scoring as low as 30%.

drillQuestions.question: The accuracy of leading language models in non-English languages was between twelve and twenty-nine ______ points lower than in English.

sentences.notes: 구체적 수치(12~29%p, 23%)를 제시하여 언어 격차의 심각성을 데이터로 입증하는 핵심 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Research published in late 2025
the accuracy of leading language models
between twelve and twenty-nine percentage points lower
scoring as low as twenty-three percent

sentences.original: Because models break text into small units called tokens, and that process is optimized for English, the same question can cost up to five times more to process in a language like Yoruba.
sentences.translation: 모델이 텍스트를 토큰이라 불리는 작은 단위로 쪼개는데, 이 과정이 영어에 최적화되어 있기 때문에, 요루바어 같은 언어에서는 같은 질문을 처리하는 데 최대 5배의 비용이 들 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: Because models break text into small units called tokens
grammarElements.label: Because 이유절 1

grammarElements.text: and that process is optimized for English
grammarElements.label: 이유절 2 (병렬)

grammarElements.text: can cost up to five times more to process
grammarElements.label: 조동사 + 비용 동사 + 비교급

vocabulary.example: Large language models process text by breaking it into tokens.
The algorithm is optimized for speed rather than accuracy.

expressions.usage: 비용 차이의 규모를 극대화하여 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Because [technical process], and [that process] is [designed for X], [result] can cost up to [multiplier] more in [alternative context]
patterns.explanation: 기술적 원인과 그로 인한 비용 격차를 설명하는 인과 패턴
examples: Because chips are designed for sequential tasks, and that architecture is optimized for CPUs, the same computation can cost up to ten times more on standard hardware.
Because search indexes are built in English, and that structure is optimized for Latin scripts, the same lookup can cost up to three times more in Arabic.

drillQuestions.question: The same question can cost up to five times more to process in a language like ______.

sentences.notes: 토큰화라는 기술적 개념을 비전문가도 이해할 수 있도록 비용 비교(5배)로 구체화한 설명 기법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: models break text into small units called tokens
koreanAlignment.ko: 모델이 텍스트를 토큰이라 불리는 작은 단위로 쪼갠다

koreanAlignment.en: that process is optimized for English
can cost up to five times more to process
koreanAlignment.ko: 처리하는 데 최대 5배의 비용이 들 수 있다

sentences.original: The language gap carries real-world stakes.
sentences.translation: 이 언어 격차는 현실적인 이해관계를 수반한다.

vocabulary.example: The stakes in this election are higher than ever before.

expressions.usage: 추상적 논의가 실제 삶에 미치는 영향이 있음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 이론적 문제를 현실적 영향으로 연결하는 전환 패턴
examples: The digital divide carries real-world stakes for rural education.
The data gap carries real-world stakes for public health policy.

sentences.notes: 짧은 주제문으로 이론적 분석에서 현실적 영향으로 전환하는 문단 도입부이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The language gap
carries real-world stakes

sentences.original: A fifty-million-dollar initiative to deploy AI tools in a thousand primary health clinics across Africa risks offering inferior medical advice simply because patients speak local languages.
sentences.translation: 아프리카 전역의 1,000개 1차 의료 클리닉에 AI 도구를 배포하려는 5,000만 달러 규모의 계획은, 단지 환자들이 현지 언어를 사용한다는 이유로 열등한 의료 조언을 제공할 위험이 있다.

grammarElements.text: A fifty-million-dollar initiative to deploy AI tools in a thousand primary health clinics across Africa
grammarElements.label: 주어 (긴 명사구 + to부정사 수식)

grammarElements.text: simply because patients speak local languages
grammarElements.label: simply because 이유절

vocabulary.example: The government launched a new initiative to combat youth unemployment.
Patients in rural areas often receive inferior care.

expressions.usage: 의도치 않은 부정적 결과의 가능성을 경고할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Large-scale initiative] risks [negative outcome] simply because [overlooked factor]
patterns.explanation: 대규모 사업이 간과된 요인 때문에 실패할 수 있음을 경고하는 패턴
examples: A billion-dollar education platform risks excluding disabled students simply because interfaces lack accessibility features.
A nationwide digital ID system risks marginalizing the elderly simply because they lack smartphones.

drillQuestions.question: The initiative risks offering ______ medical advice because patients speak local languages.

sentences.notes: simply because가 문제의 원인이 단순하지만 결과가 심각함을 강조하며, 불평등의 구조적 성격을 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: A fifty-million-dollar initiative
to deploy AI tools in a thousand primary health clinics
risks offering inferior medical advice
koreanAlignment.ko: 열등한 의료 조언을 제공할 위험이 있다

koreanAlignment.en: simply because patients speak local languages
koreanAlignment.ko: 단지 환자들이 현지 언어를 사용하기 때문에

sentences.original: Even explicitly multilingual models often retrieve facts internally in English and translate only at the final step, introducing additional errors at every stage.
sentences.translation: 명시적으로 다국어를 지원하는 모델조차 내부적으로는 영어로 사실을 검색한 뒤 최종 단계에서만 번역하므로, 각 단계에서 추가적인 오류가 발생한다.

grammarElements.text: often retrieve facts internally in English
grammarElements.label: 빈도부사 + 동사 + 부사

grammarElements.text: and translate only at the final step
grammarElements.label: 병렬 동사 2

grammarElements.text: introducing additional errors at every stage
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 결과구문

vocabulary.example: The system retrieves relevant documents from a database.
The company launched a multilingual customer service platform.

expressions.phrase: introducing additional errors at every stage
expressions.usage: 연쇄적 오류 누적을 표현할 때 사용하는 기술 분석 표현

patterns.pattern: Even [seemingly capable X] often [does A internally] and [does B] only at [final point], introducing [cumulative problem]
patterns.explanation: 겉으로 보이는 능력과 내부 작동 방식의 괴리를 폭로하는 패턴
examples: Even supposedly real-time translators often process speech in batches and output text only after a delay, introducing noticeable lag.
Even advanced recommendation engines often calculate preferences in a single language and localize only at display, introducing cultural mismatches.

drillQuestions.question: Multilingual models often ______ facts internally in English and translate only at the final step.

sentences.notes: Even으로 시작하여 다국어 모델조차 근본적으로 영어 중심이라는 기술적 한계를 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Even explicitly multilingual models
retrieve facts internally in English
translate only at the final step
introducing additional errors at every stage

sentences.original: Fine-tuning with small amounts of high-quality non-English data can improve accuracy by more than five percentage points, but the dominant economic incentives still favor English-first development.
sentences.translation: 소량의 고품질 비영어 데이터로 미세 조정하면 정확도를 5퍼센트포인트 이상 향상시킬 수 있지만, 지배적인 경제적 인센티브는 여전히 영어 우선 개발을 선호한다.

grammarElements.text: Fine-tuning with small amounts of high-quality non-English data
grammarElements.label: 동명사구 주어

grammarElements.text: can improve accuracy by more than five percentage points
grammarElements.label: 조동사 + 동사 + 정도 부사구

grammarElements.text: but the dominant economic incentives still favor English-first development
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: Fine-tuning the model on domain-specific data improved its performance significantly.
Tax incentives attracted foreign manufacturers to the region.

expressions.usage: 기술 산업의 구조적 편향을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Solution] can improve [metric] by [amount], but [structural barrier] still favors [status quo]
patterns.explanation: 해결책이 존재하지만 구조적 장벽이 이를 가로막는 현실을 대비하는 패턴
examples: Renewable subsidies can improve adoption by 20%, but fossil fuel lobbies still favor traditional energy.
Local hiring can improve diversity by 15%, but recruitment algorithms still favor established networks.

drillQuestions.question: The dominant economic ______ still favor English-first development.

sentences.notes: 해결책(fine-tuning)과 구조적 장벽(경제적 인센티브)을 but으로 대비시켜 문제의 근원이 기술이 아닌 경제 논리에 있음을 시사한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Fine-tuning with small amounts of high-quality non-English data
koreanAlignment.ko: 소량의 고품질 비영어 데이터로 미세 조정하면

koreanAlignment.en: can improve accuracy by more than five percentage points
the dominant economic incentives still favor English-first development
koreanAlignment.ko: 지배적인 경제적 인센티브는 여전히 영어 우선 개발을 선호한다

sentences.original: A parallel limitation is emerging in hardware.
sentences.translation: 이와 유사한 한계가 하드웨어 분야에서도 나타나고 있다.

vocabulary.example: A parallel debate is unfolding in the education sector.

expressions.usage: 다른 분야에서도 비슷한 문제가 발생하고 있음을 연결할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: A parallel [problem/trend] is emerging in [new domain]
patterns.explanation: 앞선 논의와 구조적으로 유사한 새 주제를 연결하는 전환 패턴
examples: A parallel concern is emerging in education.
A parallel shift is emerging in manufacturing.

drillQuestions.question: A ______ limitation is emerging in hardware.

sentences.notes: 언어 격차에서 하드웨어 한계로 주제를 전환하는 짧은 전환 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: A parallel limitation
is emerging in hardware

sentences.original: The shift from training AI models to running them in real time, a process known as inference, is exposing the weaknesses of current processors.
sentences.translation: AI 모델을 훈련시키는 것에서 실시간으로 실행하는 것으로의 전환, 즉 추론(inference)이라 불리는 과정이 현재 프로세서의 약점을 드러내고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The shift from training AI models to running them in real time
grammarElements.label: 주어 (from A to B 전환 명사구)

vocabulary.example: Running inference on edge devices requires efficient chip design.
The audit is exposing weaknesses in the firm's compliance systems.

expressions.usage: 전문 용어를 독자에게 소개할 때 쓰는 동격 표현

patterns.pattern: The shift from [A] to [B], a process known as [term], is exposing [hidden problem]
patterns.explanation: 전환 과정에서 드러나는 숨겨진 문제를 설명하며 전문 용어를 소개하는 패턴
examples: The shift from on-premise to cloud computing, a process known as migration, is exposing security gaps.
The shift from fossil fuels to renewables, a process known as energy transition, is exposing grid weaknesses.

drillQuestions.question: The shift from training to running AI models in real time is called ______.

sentences.notes: 동격 삽입구(a process known as inference)가 전문 용어를 자연스럽게 정의하며, 저널리즘에서 자주 쓰이는 기법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The shift from training AI models to running them in real time
koreanAlignment.ko: AI 모델을 훈련시키는 것에서 실시간 실행으로의 전환

koreanAlignment.en: a process known as inference
is exposing the weaknesses of current processors

sentences.original: Computing performance has roughly tripled every few years, but off-chip memory bandwidth has improved by only a factor of about 1.6, creating what engineers call a memory wall.
sentences.translation: 컴퓨팅 성능은 수 년마다 대략 3배씩 증가해 왔지만, 칩 외부 메모리 대역폭은 약 1.6배밖에 개선되지 않아 엔지니어들이 '메모리 벽'이라 부르는 현상을 만들어내고 있다.

grammarElements.text: has roughly tripled every few years
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사 + 빈도 부사구

grammarElements.text: has improved by only a factor of about 1.6
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사 + 정도 부사구

grammarElements.text: creating what engineers call a memory wall
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 결과구문 + what절

vocabulary.example: Insufficient memory bandwidth limits the speed of data-intensive applications.
Data transfer speed is the main bottleneck in the system.

expressions.phrase: what engineers call a memory wall
expressions.usage: 전문 분야의 용어를 일반 독자에게 소개할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Metric A] has [improved significantly], but [Metric B] has improved by only [modest amount], creating what [experts] call [technical term]
patterns.explanation: 두 지표의 불균형한 발전을 대비하여 구조적 문제를 설명하는 패턴
examples: Processor speeds have doubled every two years, but storage latency has improved by only 10%, creating what developers call an I/O gap.
Drug discovery speed has tripled, but clinical trial approval has improved by only 20%, creating what researchers call the regulatory lag.

drillQuestions.question: Off-chip memory ______ has improved by only a factor of about 1.6.

sentences.notes: tripled와 only 1.6의 대비가 메모리 벽 문제의 심각성을 직관적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Computing performance has roughly tripled every few years
koreanAlignment.ko: 컴퓨팅 성능은 수 년마다 대략 3배씩 증가해 왔다

koreanAlignment.en: off-chip memory bandwidth has improved by only a factor of about 1.6
koreanAlignment.ko: 칩 외부 메모리 대역폭은 약 1.6배밖에 개선되지 않았다

koreanAlignment.en: what engineers call a memory wall

sentences.original: Several startups are now designing chips that tackle this bottleneck through larger on-chip memory, in-memory computing, or architectures tailored specifically to transformer models, though the pace of algorithmic change means today's specialized hardware could quickly become obsolete.
sentences.translation: 여러 스타트업이 현재 더 큰 온칩 메모리, 인메모리 컴퓨팅, 또는 트랜스포머 모델에 특화된 아키텍처를 통해 이 병목 현상을 해결하는 칩을 설계하고 있지만, 알고리즘 변화의 속도가 빨라 오늘날의 특수 하드웨어는 금방 구식이 될 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: are now designing chips that tackle this bottleneck
grammarElements.label: 현재진행형 동사 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: through larger on-chip memory, in-memory computing, or architectures tailored specifically to transformer models
grammarElements.label: through + 세 가지 수단 (병렬)

grammarElements.text: though the pace of algorithmic change means today's specialized hardware could quickly become obsolete
grammarElements.label: though 양보절

vocabulary.example: Rapid innovation made the previous generation of chips obsolete within two years.
The curriculum is tailored to the needs of advanced learners.

expressions.usage: 핵심 기술 장벽에 도전하는 행위를 표현

patterns.pattern: Several [innovators] are now [building solution] through [method A], [method B], or [method C], though [risk] means [solution] could quickly become [outdated]
patterns.explanation: 혁신적 시도를 나열한 뒤 though로 기술 진부화 위험을 경고하는 패턴
examples: Several labs are now developing vaccines through mRNA, protein subunit, or viral vector platforms, though mutation rates mean today's formulas could quickly become ineffective.

Several firms are now building quantum computers through superconducting, trapped-ion, or photonic approaches, though the pace of theoretical advances means today's designs could quickly become obsolete.

drillQuestions.question: Today's specialized hardware could quickly become ______.

sentences.notes: 세 가지 기술적 접근법을 병렬로 나열한 뒤 though절로 기술 진부화의 역설을 제시하는 고급 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Several startups are now designing chips
koreanAlignment.ko: 여러 스타트업이 현재 칩을 설계하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: tackle this bottleneck
architectures tailored specifically to transformer models
today's specialized hardware could quickly become obsolete
koreanAlignment.ko: 오늘날의 특수 하드웨어는 금방 구식이 될 수 있다

sentences.original: Meanwhile, in India's outsourcing sector, which generated more than three hundred and fifteen billion dollars in combined revenue this year, the feared mass automation of coding has not materialized.
sentences.translation: 한편, 올해 합산 매출 3,150억 달러 이상을 기록한 인도의 아웃소싱 부문에서는 우려했던 코딩의 대규모 자동화가 실현되지 않았다.

grammarElements.text: which generated more than three hundred and fifteen billion dollars in combined revenue this year
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절

grammarElements.text: the feared mass automation of coding
grammarElements.label: 주어 (과거분사 수식 + 명사)

vocabulary.example: The expected recession never materialized.
IT outsourcing remains a major revenue source for Indian firms.

expressions.usage: 널리 예측되었던 부정적 결과가 일어나지 않았음을 표현

patterns.pattern: Meanwhile, in [sector], which generated [impressive figure], the feared [disruption] has not materialized
patterns.explanation: 규모를 강조한 뒤 예측된 위협이 실현되지 않았음을 밝히는 패턴
examples: Meanwhile, in global shipping, which moved twelve billion tons this year, the feared collapse of supply chains has not materialized.
Meanwhile, in the advertising industry, which generated five hundred billion dollars, the feared death of traditional media has not materialized.

drillQuestions.question: The feared mass automation of coding has not ______.

sentences.notes: Meanwhile가 하드웨어에서 인도 아웃소싱으로 주제를 전환하며, has not materialized가 기대와 현실의 괴리를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Meanwhile, in India's outsourcing sector
which generated more than three hundred and fifteen billion dollars
the feared mass automation of coding
has not materialized

sentences.original: Deploying AI tools in legacy software environments with poor documentation and intertwined systems remains far harder than building new applications from scratch.
sentences.translation: 문서화가 부실하고 시스템이 복잡하게 얽힌 레거시 소프트웨어 환경에 AI 도구를 배포하는 것은 새 애플리케이션을 처음부터 만드는 것보다 훨씬 더 어렵다.

grammarElements.text: Deploying AI tools in legacy software environments with poor documentation and intertwined systems
grammarElements.label: 동명사구 주어 (긴 명사구)

vocabulary.example: Migrating from legacy systems to modern platforms is expensive.
The two economies are deeply intertwined.

expressions.usage: 기존 토대 없이 완전히 새로 시작하는 것을 표현

patterns.pattern: [Doing X in messy environment] remains far harder than [doing Y in clean environment]
patterns.explanation: 현실의 복잡성이 이론적 가능성을 훨씬 초과함을 비교하는 패턴
examples: Retrofitting old buildings for energy efficiency remains far harder than designing green buildings from the ground up.
Reforming existing institutions remains far harder than establishing new ones with modern mandates.

drillQuestions.question: Deploying AI tools in legacy environments remains far harder than building new applications from ______.

sentences.notes: 동명사구 대 동명사구의 비교 구조가 이론(greenfield)과 현실(legacy)의 간극을 명확히 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Deploying AI tools in legacy software environments
koreanAlignment.ko: 레거시 소프트웨어 환경에 AI 도구를 배포하는 것

koreanAlignment.en: with poor documentation and intertwined systems
remains far harder than
building new applications from scratch

sentences.original: Outsourcing firms are repositioning as strategic AI consultants rather than code factories, and their hiring has continued despite widespread predictions of displacement.
sentences.translation: 아웃소싱 기업들은 코드 공장이 아닌 전략적 AI 컨설턴트로 스스로를 재포지셔닝하고 있으며, 대규모 일자리 대체에 대한 광범위한 예측에도 불구하고 채용은 계속되고 있다.

grammarElements.text: are repositioning as strategic AI consultants rather than code factories
grammarElements.label: 현재진행형 + as A rather than B

vocabulary.example: The brand is repositioning itself as a luxury label.
Fears of job displacement due to automation are widespread.

expressions.usage: 이전 역할과 새 역할의 대비를 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Firms] are repositioning as [new role] rather than [old role], and [positive indicator] has continued despite [negative predictions]
patterns.explanation: 산업 전환과 예측 빗나감을 동시에 보여주는 패턴
examples: Banks are repositioning as tech platforms rather than financial intermediaries, and their tech hiring has continued despite predictions of branch closures.
Media companies are repositioning as content studios rather than news outlets, and their subscriber growth has continued despite predictions of decline.

drillQuestions.question: Outsourcing firms are ______ as strategic AI consultants rather than code factories.

sentences.notes: rather than과 despite의 조합이 변화의 방향(재포지셔닝)과 결과(채용 지속)를 압축적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Outsourcing firms are repositioning as strategic AI consultants
koreanAlignment.ko: 아웃소싱 기업들은 전략적 AI 컨설턴트로 재포지셔닝하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: rather than code factories
their hiring has continued
despite widespread predictions of displacement
koreanAlignment.ko: 대규모 대체에 대한 광범위한 예측에도 불구하고

sentences.original: The common thread across these domains is that artificial intelligence excels in controlled, English-language, greenfield environments but struggles with the messy complexity of the real world.
sentences.translation: 이 모든 분야를 관통하는 공통된 실마리는, 인공지능이 통제된 영어 중심의 그린필드 환경에서는 뛰어나지만 현실 세계의 복잡한 혼란에는 고전한다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The common thread across these domains
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: artificial intelligence excels in controlled, English-language, greenfield environments
grammarElements.label: that절 내 주절

grammarElements.text: but struggles with the messy complexity of the real world
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: Building a greenfield factory is simpler than renovating an existing one.
The model excels at pattern recognition tasks.

expressions.usage: 여러 사례에서 공통 패턴을 도출할 때 사용하는 분석적 표현

patterns.pattern: The common thread across [domains] is that [subject] excels in [ideal conditions] but struggles with [real-world conditions]
patterns.explanation: 여러 사례를 하나의 통찰로 종합하는 결론 도출 패턴
examples: The common thread across these failures is that regulation excels in stable markets but struggles with rapidly evolving technologies.
The common thread across these studies is that prevention excels in controlled trials but struggles with real-world patient behavior.

drillQuestions.question: AI excels in controlled environments but ______ with the messy complexity of the real world.

sentences.notes: 글의 핵심 논제를 한 문장으로 요약하며, excels와 struggles의 대비가 글 전체의 결론을 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The common thread across these domains
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 모든 분야를 관통하는 공통된 실마리

koreanAlignment.en: excels in controlled, English-language, greenfield environments
koreanAlignment.ko: 통제된 영어 중심의 그린필드 환경에서 뛰어나다

koreanAlignment.en: struggles with the messy complexity of the real world

pronunciationNotes: greenfield: /ˈɡriːnfiːld/ '그린필드' — IT/비즈니스에서 '기존 제약 없이 새로 시작하는' 의미

sentences.original: Closing these gaps will require not just better models but more diverse training data, more efficient hardware, and sustained investment in the populations and systems that current technology serves least well.
sentences.translation: 이러한 격차를 좁히려면 더 나은 모델뿐만 아니라 더 다양한 훈련 데이터, 더 효율적인 하드웨어, 그리고 현재 기술이 가장 제대로 서비스하지 못하는 인구와 시스템에 대한 지속적인 투자가 필요할 것이다.

grammarElements.text: not just better models but more diverse training data, more efficient hardware, and sustained investment
grammarElements.label: not just A but B, C, and D (목적어 병렬)

grammarElements.text: in the populations and systems that current technology serves least well
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Sustained investment in education pays off over decades.
The team needs more diverse perspectives to solve the problem.

expressions.usage: 기대되는 해결책을 넘어 추가적 요구 사항을 제시할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Closing [gaps] will require not just [obvious solution] but [additional requirement 1], [requirement 2], and [requirement 3]
patterns.explanation: 단순한 해결책을 넘어 다층적 대응이 필요함을 강조하는 결론 패턴
examples: Closing the skills gap will require not just better training but deeper employer engagement, flexible credentials, and funding for lifelong learning.
Closing the climate gap will require not just cleaner energy but smarter grids, carbon capture, and behavioral change.

drillQuestions.question: Closing these gaps will require not just better models but more diverse training data, more efficient hardware, and ______ investment.

sentences.notes: not just ... but 구조로 해결책의 복합성을 강조하며, 마지막 that절이 글의 윤리적 메시지를 담은 결론이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Closing these gaps will require
not just better models but more diverse training data
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 나은 모델뿐만 아니라 더 다양한 훈련 데이터

koreanAlignment.en: sustained investment
the populations and systems that current technology serves least well
koreanAlignment.ko: 현재 기술이 가장 제대로 서비스하지 못하는 인구와 시스템
